semantics_note: membership and encoding are orthogonal axes; an output can be CLAIMS+CANONICAL inside a MALFORMED transaction; unlisted outputs are IGNORED; reason_code is the FIRST failing Section 3 rule.
synthetic_vector_warning: These are synthetic transaction serializations built from the donor's input bytes. They are not asserted to be valid spends of the donor UTXO and must not be broadcast or used as Bitcoin inclusion/Merkle fixtures. Grammar verification only.

vectors.label: zero claimants (payment, no marker)
vectors.notes: no output claims the magic

vectors.label: vout intended 1 encoded big-endian
vectors.notes: 00 00 00 01 decodes unsigned LE to 16777216 -> out of range

vectors.label: vout == outputCount
vectors.notes: 2 outputs, vout=2 not < outputCount

vectors.label: vout == markerOutputIndex
vectors.notes: marker at index 0 names vout=0 (itself)

vectors.label: vout == 0xffffffff
vectors.notes: high-bit-set vout decoded unsigned -> far out of range

vectors.label: nonzero value on canonical marker
vectors.notes: canonical script but marker output value = 1 satoshi

vectors.label: named output fails; another output qualifies
vectors.notes: anyOutputMatches killer: only the NAMED output counts

vectors.label: named output right script, amount too low
vectors.notes: committed script, value 19999 < 20000

vectors.label: named output sufficient amount, wrong script
vectors.notes: value 20000 but wrong script at the named vout

vectors.label: second claimant at last of 300 outputs
vectors.notes: 300 outputs; hidden second claimant last -> full scan catches it

vectors.label: one claimant, 300 outputs (at capacity)
vectors.notes: 300 outputs == assumed scan capacity; valid

vectors.label: one claimant, 301 outputs (over capacity)
vectors.notes: 301 > assumed scan capacity 300; grammar valid but capacity-gated

vectors.label: two canonical claimants, same vault_id
vectors.notes: two canonical markers -> ambiguity

vectors.label: two canonical claimants, different vault_ids
vectors.notes: two canonical markers naming different vaults -> ambiguity

vectors.label: wrong payload length 42
vectors.notes: direct push of 42 bytes; claims but non-canonical

vectors.label: wrong payload length 44
vectors.notes: direct push of 44 bytes; claims but non-canonical

vectors.label: declared 43 but physically truncated
vectors.notes: declared 43, only 40 payload bytes; truncation rule -> claims

vectors.label: declared 43 with a trailing byte
vectors.notes: canonical push plus one trailing byte; non-canonical

vectors.label: declared 43 with a trailing second push
vectors.notes: canonical push plus a trailing second push; non-canonical

vectors.label: PUSHDATA1 encoding alone
vectors.notes: correct payload via PUSHDATA1; non-canonical claimant

vectors.label: PUSHDATA2 encoding alone
vectors.notes: correct payload via PUSHDATA2; non-canonical claimant

vectors.label: PUSHDATA4 encoding alone
vectors.notes: correct payload via PUSHDATA4; non-canonical claimant

vectors.label: canonical + PUSHDATA1 -> two claimants
vectors.notes: re-encoded second claimant cannot smuggle past exactly-one

vectors.label: canonical + PUSHDATA2 -> two claimants
vectors.notes: re-encoded second claimant cannot smuggle past exactly-one

vectors.label: canonical + PUSHDATA4 -> two claimants
vectors.notes: re-encoded second claimant cannot smuggle past exactly-one

vectors.label: PUSHDATA1 declared 0 then magic (ignored)
vectors.notes: declared length 0 < 4 -> does not claim

vectors.label: PUSHDATA1 declared 1 then magic (ignored)
vectors.notes: declared length 1 < 4 -> does not claim

vectors.label: PUSHDATA1 declared 2 then magic (ignored)
vectors.notes: declared length 2 < 4 -> does not claim

vectors.label: PUSHDATA1 declared 3 then magic (ignored)
vectors.notes: declared length 3 < 4 -> does not claim

vectors.label: declared >=4 magic present, push beyond slice
vectors.notes: PUSHDATA1 declared 43, only 4 physical magic bytes; claims (trunc)

vectors.label: slice ends before four magic bytes (ignored)
vectors.notes: direct push declared 5, only 3 physical bytes; magic incomplete

vectors.label: incomplete PUSHDATA1 length prefix (ignored)
vectors.notes: no length byte after 0x4c -> no decodable push

vectors.label: incomplete PUSHDATA2 length prefix (ignored)
vectors.notes: only 1 of 2 length bytes after 0x4d -> no decodable push

vectors.label: incomplete PUSHDATA4 length prefix (ignored)
vectors.notes: only 3 of 4 length bytes after 0x4e -> no decodable push

vectors.label: PUSHDATA4 length 0x80000004 with magic (claims)
vectors.notes: unsigned decode keeps 0x80000004 positive; claims, malformed

vectors.label: empty first push then magic second push
vectors.notes: OP_0 empty first push; magic in second push -> ignored

vectors.label: unrelated first push then magic second push
vectors.notes: non-magic first push; magic in second push -> ignored

vectors.label: magic embedded in P2WSH program
vectors.notes: 0x00 0x20 witness v0 program; not OP_RETURN -> ignored

vectors.label: magic embedded in P2TR program
vectors.notes: 0x51 0x20 witness v1 program; not OP_RETURN -> ignored

vectors.label: script not starting 0x6a
vectors.notes: P2PKH-shaped, magic in hash160; first byte != 0x6a -> ignored

vectors.label: near-miss magic ERGW
vectors.notes: canonical framing but magic ERGW != ERGV -> ignored

vectors.label: near-miss magic ERGU
vectors.notes: canonical framing but magic ERGU != ERGV -> ignored

vectors.label: near-miss magic ergv (lowercase)
vectors.notes: canonical framing but magic ergv != ERGV -> ignored

vectors.label: unknown version 0x02
vectors.notes: canonical framing, version byte mutated to 0x02

vectors.label: ergo_net wrong (0x02 testnet)
vectors.notes: ergo_net = testnet vs mainnet deployment

vectors.label: btc_net wrong (0x02 Testnet4)
vectors.notes: btc_net = Testnet4 vs mainnet deployment

vectors.label: ergo_net unlisted enum 0xff
vectors.notes: ergo_net unlisted value -> malformed

vectors.label: btc_net unlisted enum 0xff
vectors.notes: btc_net unlisted value -> malformed

vectors.label: vault_id = sha256(EXPECTED_VAULT_ID)
vectors.notes: unrelated 32-byte id (sha256 of the expected id); no flip/reversal relation

vectors.label: vault_id one-byte mismatch
vectors.notes: first vault_id byte flipped by one bit

vectors.label: vault_id full byte reversal
vectors.notes: reversed id (the display-order-confusion counterexample)

vectors.label: canonical marker first, payment after
vectors.notes: marker at index 0, vout=1 names the payment

vectors.label: canonical marker middle, payment before
vectors.notes: marker at index 1, vout=0 names the earlier payment

vectors.label: canonical marker last, payment before
vectors.notes: marker at index 1 (last), vout=0 names the payment

vectors.label: canonical marker middle, payment after
vectors.notes: marker at index 1, vout=2 names the later payment

vectors.label: unrelated OP_RETURN before canonical marker
vectors.notes: donor's real OP_RETURN (non-magic) ignored; marker at 1, vout=2

vectors.label: unrelated OP_RETURN after canonical marker
vectors.notes: unrelated OP_RETURN after marker ignored; marker at 0, vout=1

vectors.label: two qualifying payments, named vout is one
vectors.notes: outputs 0 and 2 both qualify; vout=0 names one -> valid

vectors.label: vout == outputCount-1 (upper boundary)
vectors.notes: 3 outputs, vout=2 = outputCount-1, payment at 2

vectors.label: vout=1 encoded 01 00 00 00 (LE control)
vectors.notes: vout packed little-endian 01000000; payment at index 1

vectors.label: vout=256 (00 01 00 00), 257 outputs
vectors.notes: 257 outputs within scan capacity; payment at index 256
